title: Peloton Q3 FY2026 Slides: Revenue Returns to Growth, Stock Surges 12%
text: Peloton Q3 2026 Earnings Results and Market Reaction. Revenue Growth Return: Peloton reported Q3 FY2026 revenue of $631 million, exceeding guidance of $605-625M and analyst expectations of $618.74M. This marked the first year-over-year revenue growth Peloton has reported in some time. Market Response: Following the earnings announcement, Peloton's stock surged 12.53% in pre-market trading, reflecting investor optimism despite the EPS miss. EPS Performance: Earnings per share of $0.06 fell short of the $0.07 forecast, showing the company still has profitability challenges despite revenue growth. Profitability Metrics: Adjusted EBITDA reached $126 million (up 41% YoY), while free cash flow climbed to $151 million (up 59% YoY), indicating strong operational improvements. Balance Sheet: Net debt decreased significantly to $173 million, down 70% from $585 million annually. Guidance Update: The company updated its fiscal 2026 guidance to $2.42-2.44 billion in revenue with adjusted EBITDA of $470-480 million. Strategic Optionality: Management emphasized the company now has strategic optionality with reduced debt, enabling potential debt refinancing, share repurchases, and targeted investments.
